text.title: compaction-preserve-refs Decision Tree
text.summary: Decision tree routing the agent from compaction trigger to compaction strategy.

decision-tree.root-question: Is context utilization above the compaction threshold (default 0.85) OR conversation length above the configured turn cap?

branch.conclusion: Do not compact yet; methodology does not apply this turn.

branch.question: Will a downstream session (next turn, next process restart, next agent in the mesh) read this state and resume work?

branch.conclusion: Use plain conversation summary; this methodology's full schema is overkill for display-only summaries.

branch.question: Are large tool outputs already offloaded to the agent's filesystem (per filesystem-as-working-memory)?

branch.conclusion: Run full six-key compaction; refs point at the on-disk artifacts, decisions and errors_open carry verbatim strings.

branch.question: Does the conversation contain raw tool output bodies above ~2000 tokens?

branch.conclusion: Offload bodies to filesystem first (delegating to filesystem-as-working-memory), then run the six-key compaction so refs can point at the freshly-written paths.
Run full six-key compaction directly; no offload step needed.

text.example: q-trigger → branch(utilization >= 0.85) → q-resume-needed → branch(true) → q-offload-available → branch(true) → conclusion(r3-fixed-schema).
